opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My skin is so tight and thick it feels like it's going to tear open if I move. My elbows and knees are bleeding continuously, and the pain is unbearable. (Patient's spouse is with him and adds that the skin flared up massively over the last week.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I've had psoriasis for years but over the last week it has exploded all over my body. The thick scaly patches have cracked open, they bleed whenever I bend my arms or legs, and it hurts so much I can barely move — I've had to stay in bed. I need something for the pain and to stop the cracking.

symptom_narrative.onset: a sudden, explosive worsening over the past 7 days, on top of psoriasis I've had for about 10 years
symptom_narrative.duration: about a week of severe flare
symptom_narrative.character: thick, raised, red patches covered in dense silvery-white flakes; the skin over them feels rigid and tight, and it splits into deep painful cracks that bleed when I move
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the pain is unbearable, especially when I bend my elbows or knees; the tightness makes me feel like the skin will tear
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: mainly my elbows, knees, lower back and scalp, but it has spread across my trunk too
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant; much worse with any movement that stretches the cracked skin
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: keeping completely still; nothing else has helped
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving, bending my joints, and the skin drying out and tightening
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: continuous bleeding from the cracks; a new patch came up where I scratched my forearm a few days ago; I feel otherwise well in myself, no fevers or shakes
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had psoriasis flares before but never anything this severe or this widespread
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I've been stuck in bed because it's too painful to move my arms and legs

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: psoriasis (usually just on my elbows and knees)
past_medical_history.how_managed: a prescribed cream I put on the patches

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a calcipotriol/steroid ointment for my psoriasis

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: has been under a lot of stress recently, and had a bad throat infection a couple of weeks ago that seemed to set this off; spouse is helping care for him at home while he is bed-bound

objective_data_offered.value: widespread thick, raised red patches covered with dense silvery-white flakes over the elbows, knees, lower back and scalp, and spread across the trunk; deep cracks in the skin at the creases of the elbows and behind the knees that bleed when he moves; lots of silver flakes shedding onto the bedding
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: seen only over a phone video in variable home lighting; the doctor cannot touch, stretch, scrape or measure the skin

objective_data_offered.value: the red scaly patches are discrete plaques with clear edges and there is normal-coloured skin in between them; his skin is NOT red all over the whole body, and there are no pus-filled blisters or yellow weeping/oozing visible on video
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: a video cannot reliably judge total body-surface-area involvement or detect early pustules/secondary infection

objective_data_offered.value: no fever, feels a normal temperature, no shivering or shaking chills
alert and talking normally but visibly in pain, lying very still to avoid bending the cracked joints
